notes: SHIP v1.14.270 - SHIP A: DATA HONESTY (RULING R2 cables sourcing + R3 _rmConnHit untangle; per RULINGS-INSPECT3D-NIGHT-PACK.md, owner-ruled 'r2 yes, r4 yes' + 'ship a first'). Ships FIRST because it corrects a LIVE FABRICATION. ==== R2 - CABLES ARE NOW SOURCED FROM THE MASTER. The two hardcoded loops are DELETED. They drew 22 tubes of INVENTED cable on EVERY rack: the U-pairs (40->31, 40->30, 22->13) were literals inherited verbatim from MOCKUP-INSPECT3D-FINAL.html in .256; nothing traced to slots[]. THE KILL SHOT: totalU defaults to 48, so every literal fell inside [1,48] and the 'bound the mock's hardcoded rows to THIS rack' guard culled NOTHING - an empty rack and a full rack drew the identical runs. Reachable in two taps (3D -> CABLES), no flag beyond redesign_isOn (the default UI), wearing the app's own accent palette, with no legend and no label: the only honesty was a code comment the tech never sees. A cable map is a WORK INSTRUCTION, so this was the same class as fabricated telemetry and more dangerous than a wrong dashboard number. Second tell: the mock's cx reached +10.0 on a rack spanning +/-3.0, so five of its eight columns rendered OUTSIDE the rack body - they were never fitted to anything. NOW: createCable() (the .256 renderer) is UNTOUCHED - only the data feed changed. Every tube is a real Master A/Z cable row whose BOTH endpoints resolve to slots in THIS rack. Cross-rack links are NOT drawn (a half-tube to nowhere is worse than an honest count) - they surface as a count. JOIN reuses PRE-FLIGHT's own normalizer _rec_norm against the same store _rmRenderConnections reads (master_findRack -> cablesOut/cablesIn) - no second resolver invented, per the ruling. Spread is bounded INSIDE the rack body (span = RW-1.0). ==== THE TRAP THAT WOULD HAVE DOUBLED EVERY TUBE: when aRack === zRack the Master parser pushes the SAME cable object into this rack's cablesOut AND its cablesIn (:28475-76), so a naive cablesOut.concat(cablesIn) draws every in-rack run TWICE. Fix: iterate cablesOut for in-rack runs; the cablesIn pass SKIPS any cable whose both ends resolve here. Verified live with a seed carrying those cables in BOTH arrays: 2 tubes, not 4. ==== HONEST STATES (all three driven live): in-rack runs -> 'CABLES . N EXT' enabled; rows exist but none drawable in-rack -> 'N EXT LINKS' disabled+dimmed; no rows / no Master match -> 'NO CABLE DATA' disabled. Visible-but-disabled, never hidden (Cold Aisle: the tech should see the capability exists AND why it is off). Most racks may have no cable rows at first - that is correct, not a bug. Labels are deliberately SHORT: .reh-3d-toggle is a display:flex strip with NO wrap and NO overflow guard, so prose there would push the viewport (Rule 1); the full sentence rides title/aria-label at zero width. ==== R3 - _rmConnHit UNTANGLED. The Bay tap set a STUB {id} which starved _rmRenderConnections (it reads hit.cablesOut/cablesIn), so the CONNECTIONS panel silently VANISHED when the device sheet was opened from the Bay while the same sheet from the Master path showed it. Two consumers, one global: PRINT ASSET LABEL (:41567) reads hit.id for cab context; CONNECTIONS reads the cable arrays. master_findRack returns BOTH - but the fix keeps the rack's OWN human designation as .id (never the Master's spelling) so PRINT is byte-identical; only the cable arrays are added. No Master match -> the original stub, so PRINT still works and CONNECTIONS omits itself honestly. Reuses the same exact-match bridge the elevation RESOLVE path uses (:40649). ==== VERIFIED LIVE (real app over http, Master-shaped seed): all 3 chip states; dedupe 2-not-4; device sheet renders CONNECTIONS . 2 (Et1 -> gpu-node-01 : OSFP0 MMA1B00-C002 + Et3 -> spine-01 : Et9 MFA1A00-C010) with _rmConnHit.id = C12-R07 (PRINT preserved). The design coheres: the external link CABLES refuses to draw IS visible in CONNECTIONS - exactly where the chip's tooltip sends the tech. ?legacy=1 proven clean LIVE: rackElevation_render3D called 0 times, no FLAT|3D strip, no CABLES chip, no #reh3dCanvasHost, _rmConnHit undefined, host #ops-content - all new code lives inside rackElevation_render3D, reachable only via the _rehRd-gated toggle (:33481); new CSS is body.rd-scoped. NOT verified here (needs the device): the raycast TAP firing the R3 lines - the Bay's render loop and reh3d_restore are rAF-driven and rAF never fires in a hidden automation tab; everything downstream of the hit is proven. RACK SCENE: no materials/lights/fog/tone-mapping/tray-geometry/type-colour/floor/reflection/boot change - the deleted cables are the ONLY scene delta, owner-approved ('r2 yes'). Camera untouched (that is Ship B). Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS 12 blocks balanced; CRLF uniform; three-stamp .269->.270. John iPhone gate: [1] rack WITH cable rows in Master -> 3D -> CABLES: tubes match the Master A/Z rows, count them against the sheet; NO tube on any U-pair not in Master. [2] rack WITHOUT cable rows -> chip disabled 'NO CABLE DATA', zero tubes. [3] any rack: the old ghost pattern (dense runs around U40->U31 / U22->U13) is GONE. [4] tap a tray in 3D -> sheet shows CONNECTIONS, same as the Master path; PRINT ASSET LABEL correct from BOTH paths. [5] ?legacy=1 byte-identical.
